name: Big White Soft Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a measuring cup, mix the buttermilk and baking soda; set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the oil, sugar, and eggs; mix well.
3. Add the buttermilk-soda mixture to the oil mixture and blend.
4. Add the salt, nutmeg, vanilla, and baking powder; mix again.
5. Blend in the flour (the batter will be very runny). Cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F.
7. For best results, use ungreased non-stick baking sheets.
8. Using about 1 heaping tablespoon of batter per cookie, place them on the sheets.
9. Sprinkle each cookie generously with sugar and dot with raisins.
10. Bake for just 5 minutes. The cookies should be barely done—still almost white. If golden, remove from oven.
11. Allow cookies to remain on the sheet for 3 more minutes to continue baking.
12. Carefully transfer cookies with a metal spatula to a wax paper-covered rack to cool.
13. Store cookies in tightly covered containers or freeze. Each cookie should be wrapped individually or between layers of wax paper to prevent sticking.
